steps: Go to https://portal.cdp.coinbase.com and sign in or create a Coinbase account
Enable Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) if not already enabled — it is required
Click 'Create API Key' in the top-right corner of the API Keys page
Under 'API Key permissions', ensure 'Coinbase Exchange' access is selected
Set a nickname and a passphrase (you will need this passphrase for every signed request)
Copy the API Key, Secret Key, and Passphrase — the Secret Key is shown only once

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: API Key
credential_fields.description: Your Coinbase Exchange API key (CB-ACCESS-KEY header)

credential_fields.name: api_secret
credential_fields.label: API Secret
credential_fields.description: Your EC private key for signing requests (CB-ACCESS-SIGN). Shown only once at creation.

credential_fields.name: api_passphrase
credential_fields.label: Passphrase
credential_fields.description: The passphrase you set when creating the key (CB-ACCESS-PASSPHRASE)

notes: Requires a Coinbase account with 2FA enabled
The secret key is an EC private key shown only once — save it immediately
All authenticated requests must include CB-ACCESS-KEY, CB-ACCESS-SIGN, CB-ACCESS-TIMESTAMP, and CB-ACCESS-PASSPHRASE headers
Public endpoints like /products and /currencies do not require authentication
New API keys can take up to 48 hours to become fully active
Withdrawal permissions are disabled by default for security

test_endpoint.description: List all available trading pairs — this is a public endpoint that requires no authentication
